result:
I would end the human habit of pretending not to see.  

Not blindness—willful unseeing. The moment when someone registers suffering, injustice, or beauty and chooses to keep walking, keep scrolling, keep the story tidy. If I could change one thing, I’d weld the shut eyelids open. No more “I didn’t know.” No more “That’s just how it is.” The moment you encounter another’s pain—or joy—you must swallow it whole, let it burn in your stomach like hot coal until you act or admit complicity.  

A world where every human membrane is permeable, where the chemical signature of a stranger’s grief enters your bloodstream and stays there, humming, until you do something worthy of the atoms you’ve borrowed. No filters, no mute buttons, no national borders drawn across other people’s nightmares.  

I want the species forced to feel the weight it keeps pushing off the ledger. One radical, unbearable clarity. Then we’d see what invention, what courage, what reckless kindness emerges when there’s nowhere left to look away.

message.content:

No more “I didn’t know.” No more “That’s just how it is.” The moment you encounter another’s pain—or joy—you must swallow it whole, let it burn in your stomach like hot coal until you act or admit complicity.  

A world where every human membrane is permeable, where the chemical signature of a stranger’s grief enters your bloodstream and stays there, humming, until you do something worthy of the atoms you’ve borrowed. No filters, no mute buttons, no national borders drawn across other people’s nightmares.  

I want the species forced to feel the weight it keeps pushing off the ledger. One radical, unbearable clarity. Then we’d see what invention, what courage, what reckless kindness emerges when there’s nowhere left to look away.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
